paper_title: Seed set and viability measurement in Sagittaria species

methods.description: This method investigates the effect of interspecific pollen deposition between S. pygmaea and S. trifolia through controlled hand-pollination experiments.

steps.description: Pollinations were carried out from August to September in 2019 and 2020 using approximately 140 individuals as pollen recipients.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct hand-pollination experiments with S. pygmaea and S. trifolia

steps.description: Each newly opened female flower of the day was randomly assigned one of the two pollination treatments.
steps.searchable_summary: assign pollination treatments to female flowers

steps.description: Pollen was collected and pooled from 3 to 5 plants per species by gently tapping a fully opened male flower over a Petri dish.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and pool pollen from male flowers

steps.description: Hand pollinations were performed using a small paintbrush loaded with fresh pollen grains to evenly brush the entire gynoecium.
After 2-3 weeks, we collected the mature aggregate fruits, counted the carpels per aggregate fruit, and observed the morphology of embryos under a stereoscope.
steps.searchable_summary: collect and analyze mature aggregate fruits

steps.description: Mature fruits and seeds were photographed with a digital camera.
The height and width of the aggregate fruits and achenes were measured using a digital caliper and ImageJ.
steps.searchable_summary: measure dimensions of fruits and achenes

steps.description: To compare germination rates, seeds were cold-stratified, moved to a growth chamber to germinate, and monitored for signs of germination over 14 days.

methods.method_name: Plant Cultivation and Study Site Preparation
methods.description: This method describes the cultivation of Sagittaria pygmaea and S. trifolia seedlings and the preparation of the study site for experiments.

steps.description: Seedlings were collected from monospecific populations in Wuhan, Hubei Province, China.
Seedlings were cultivated with daily watering and fertilizer application once every 2 weeks in an outdoor experimental site.
steps.searchable_summary: cultivate seedlings with watering and fertilization
